title: rubus × rhombifoliatus
intro: Rubus × rhombifoliatus, also known as the Japanese black raspberry, is a hybrid plant belonging to the Rosaceae family, resulting from the cross between Rubus fruticosus and Rubus idaeus. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high anthocyanin content, which has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rubus × rhombifoliatus include its potential to reduce cardiovascular disease risk, alleviate oxidative stress, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. Rubus × rhombifoliatus can be prepared into various herbal products, such as tea, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed for its medicinal and health benefits.
